title: pelargonium × ludovicae
intro: Pelargonium × ludovicae, also known as Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species in the geranium family, combining the characteristics of Pelargonium graveolens and Pelargonium capitatum. It is widely used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, derived from the presence of geraniol and linalool. The health benefits of Rose Geranium include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Pelargonium × ludovicae include teas, essential oils, and tinctures, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
